title: init.ts: scaffold default fabric-config.json + remove sentinel-write path
description: Two coupled init.ts changes: (1) Make fabric-config.json discoverable by writing a default file (only if absent) during init scaffolding so users can see and edit every supported config field. The default content lists every field consumed by any reader (fabric-config.ts schema + fabric-hint.cjs DEFAULT_* constants), so nothing is hidden behind silent default-on-missing. Idempotent under --reapply (never overwrite user edits). (2) Remove maybeWriteImportSentinel function, its main-flow call site, the associated clack confirm prompt ('下次开 AI 时让我从 git log 抽更多知识吗?'), and any now-unused @clack/prompts imports — coordinated with TASK-002 and TASK-004 to fully retire the sentinel mechanism.

criteria: writeDefaultFabricConfig(fabricDir) helper exists in init.ts and writes .fabric/fabric-config.json ONLY when the file is absent (mtime/exists check, no merge)

The default JSON content explicitly lists every field consumed by any reader, with the matching DEFAULT value: knowledge_language='match-existing' (per fabric-config.ts schema; treated as the production default — confirm against init/doctor behaviour before merge), archive_hint_hours=24, review_hint_pending_count=10, review_hint_pending_age_days=7, maintenance_hint_days=14, maintenance_hint_cooldown_days=7, archive_hint_cooldown_hours=12, archive_edit_threshold=20, underseed_node_threshold=10

Implementation includes a comment block referencing packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts as the source of truth for the field list, plus an instruction for future-maintainers: 'when adding a new reader, add the field here too'
Helper is invoked from the init scaffold path AND from the --reapply path; both paths are idempotent (re-runs do not overwrite an existing fabric-config.json even if fields are missing)
maybeWriteImportSentinel function, its main-flow call, and the associated clack confirm prompt are removed
No .fabric/.import-requested file is ever written by init.ts after this change
Unused @clack/prompts imports (confirm, isCancel) are removed if and only if no other init.ts code path still uses them

Updated init test files cover: (a) fresh init creates fabric-config.json with all listed fields and JSON.parse succeeds; (b) existing fabric-config.json is preserved unchanged across init re-runs (incl. --reapply); (c) no .import-requested artifact is created; (d) all prior maybeWriteImportSentinel-specific tests are removed
All other pre-existing init tests pass without modification
convergence.definition_of_done: After a fresh `fabric init`, the user finds a self-documenting .fabric/fabric-config.json on disk; running init again preserves their edits; no sentinel artifact is produced anywhere.

files.target: init scaffold path, --reapply path, maybeWriteImportSentinel + its caller + associated clack confirm + clack imports if dead
files.change: Add writeDefaultFabricConfig(fabricDir) helper that writes a JSON object with all known config fields if and only if the file does not exist. Wire it into both the scaffold and --reapply paths. Delete maybeWriteImportSentinel, its call site, the clack confirm prompt that prefaces it, and any now-unused @clack/prompts imports.

Add cases for: (a) fresh init writes fabric-config.json with all expected keys; (b) existing fabric-config.json is preserved across re-run; (c) no .fabric/.import-requested file is created. Delete sentinel-related test cases.
Confirm fabric-config.json is preserved on guarded re-runs; delete sentinel guard cases.
Ensure fabric-config.json is part of the atomic init artifact set; sentinel-related write atomicity cases are removed.
Remove assertions that the clack confirm prompt for sentinel was shown; ensure no other prompt regressions.

implementation: 1. Read packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts and packages/cli/templates/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs to enumerate every config field consumed today (cross-check with `grep _readConfigNumber` and reader functions). Final list (per investigation above): knowledge_language, archive_hint_hours, archive_hint_cooldown_hours, review_hint_pending_count, review_hint_pending_age_days, maintenance_hint_days, maintenance_hint_cooldown_days, archive_edit_threshold, underseed_node_threshold.

2. Verify each DEFAULT value against the schema and against init/doctor in-tree behaviour (special attention to knowledge_language — schema default is 'match-existing'; confirm this is the right user-facing default for a fresh repo).

3. Implement `function writeDefaultFabricConfig(fabricDir: string): void` in init.ts. Inside: target = path.join(fabricDir, 'fabric-config.json'); if (existsSync(target)) return; const defaults = { ...explicit fields with values... }; writeFileSync(target, JSON.stringify(defaults, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf8'). Add a leading JSDoc citing fabric-config.ts as source-of-truth.
4. Wire into init scaffold path (after .fabric directory creation, before any subsequent 'init complete' messaging) and into --reapply path (same idempotency semantics).

5. Locate and delete: maybeWriteImportSentinel function, its call site in main init flow, the preceding clack confirm prompt ('下次开 AI 时让我从 git log 抽更多知识吗?'). Remove @clack/prompts confirm/isCancel imports IF and ONLY IF no other code path still consumes them (grep before deleting).
6. Update tests as described in `files` block: add 3 new positive cases, remove all sentinel-specific assertions across the init test suite.
7. Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/cli test -- init` and verify the full init suite passes.

manual_checks: Run `fabric init` in a fresh tmp dir; cat .fabric/fabric-config.json and confirm every field is present and JSON-valid
Edit fabric-config.json (e.g. change review_hint_pending_count to 25); run `fabric init --reapply`; confirm the edit is preserved
success_metrics: fabric-config.json field count >= 9 (all reader-consumed fields enumerated)
Zero overwrites on re-run (file mtime preserved)
Zero .import-requested artifacts ever produced by init

regression: All non-sentinel init tests pass unchanged
init wizard flow remains interactive in identical places except the removed sentinel confirm
doctor and other downstream commands that read fabric-config.json continue to find expected values

reference.pattern: Idempotent default-file scaffolding with explicit field enumeration (no merge, no overwrite)
reference.examples: Mirror the idempotency style of other init scaffold helpers in init.ts (test for existence first, then write atomically).

rationale.chosen_approach: Write an explicit default file with every field listed; avoid silent default-on-missing in readers as the only discoverability path. Keep idempotent on re-run so user edits are sacred.
alternatives_considered: Generate a commented YAML config — rejected: codebase already standardises on JSON for .fabric/* and adding YAML would inflate dep tree
Write only the fields the user is most likely to tune — rejected: defeats the discoverability goal; user has no signal that other fields exist
Lazy-write on first reader access — rejected: scatters file-creation logic across multiple hook entry points and complicates concurrency
decision_factors: Discoverability (every field visible after fresh init)
Single source of truth (init.ts is the only writer)
Coordinated cleanup of dead sentinel code
rationale.tradeoffs: If a new field is added later, init.ts must be updated too (caught by the in-code comment + by the test that asserts known field count).

risks.description: knowledge_language default 'match-existing' may not be the right user-facing default (some downstream code may special-case this value)
risks.mitigation: Before merge, grep for knowledge_language consumers and confirm 'match-existing' produces the expected behaviour in a fresh repo (likely defaults to zh-CN or system locale at runtime)
risks.fallback: If 'match-existing' is wrong for fresh repos, write the resolved value explicitly (e.g. 'zh-CN') and document the resolution rule in the file's comment header

risks.description: Removing @clack/prompts confirm import breaks another init code path that still uses it
risks.mitigation: Grep init.ts for `confirm(`/`isCancel(` usages before removing the imports
risks.fallback: Keep the imports if any non-sentinel call remains

risks.description: Existing init tests asserting full artifact set may fail because they hardcode the file list
risks.mitigation: Update those assertions to include fabric-config.json and exclude .import-requested

key_functions.purpose: Write .fabric/fabric-config.json with all known reader-consumed fields, only if file does not exist

commit.message_template: feat(init): scaffold default fabric-config.json + remove sentinel write path
